Summary: The study introduces PCMM-Net, a deep learning framework integrating clinico-radiological features and multi-modal MRI for LVI prediction in breast cancer, demonstrating improved diagnostic performance compared to clinico-radiological features alone, MM-Net, and radiomics.

Condition1.reason: The study includes manual segmentation of the tumor volume of interest (VOI) on MRI images.

Condition2.reason: The segmentation was performed manually by a radiologist, so it is not fully automated.

Condition3.reason: The study extracts hand-crafted radiomic features using PyRadiomics.

Condition4.reason: The study includes tabular data in the form of clinico-radiological features and radiomics features.

Condition5.reason: The study uses an end-to-end deep learning model (PCMM-Net) that directly processes image data to predict LVI.

Item1.reason: The study does not explicitly mention adherence to specific radiomics or machine learning checklists or guidelines like the CLEAR checklist.

Item2.reason: The study defines inclusion and exclusion criteria for patient selection, aiming for a representative study population.

Item3.reason: The reference standard is histopathological evaluation of surgical specimens, which is a high-quality reference standard.

Item4.reason: The study uses data from a single institution.

Item5.reason: The imaging data source is MRI, which has established standardization approaches like BI-RADS.

Item6.reason: The study reports the image acquisition protocol with parameters for the MRI sequences used.

Item7.reason: The MRI examination was performed within 14 days of the surgery, which is appropriate for validating the presence or absence of LVI at the time of imaging.

Item8.reason: The study describes the manual delineation of the 3D VOI around the tumor on transverse cT1WI images using 3D Slicer.

Item9.reason: The segmentation was performed manually, not fully automatically.

Item10.reason: The segmentation was performed by a single radiologist.

Item11.reason: The study mentions using z-score normalization for feature values, which is an appropriate preprocessing technique. They also mention setting values less than zero to zero and scaling values above zero to 0-1 for DCE-MRI scans.

Item12.reason: The study used PyRadiomics for feature extraction.

Item13.reason: The study describes the feature types (first order, intensity histogram statistics, 2D and 3D shape descriptors, texture features) and mentions using a default configuration statement.

Item14.reason: The study performs univariate analysis and correlation analysis to remove redundant features.

Item15.reason: The study uses LASSO for feature selection and regularization to reduce dimensionality.

Item16.reason: The study does not explicitly discuss the appropriateness of dimensionality compared to data size or provide statistical justification.

Item17.reason: The study does not assess the robustness of the end-to-end deep learning pipeline in a test-retest setting or with image perturbations.

Item18.reason: The study mentions using random stratified sampling to create training and validation datasets.

Item19.reason: The study analyzes clinico-radiological features and performs univariate and multivariate logistic regression to identify independent predictors, addressing potential confounding factors.

Item20.reason: The study reports AUC, accuracy, sensitivity, and specificity for model evaluation and uses ROC curves.

Item21.reason: The study does not include uncertainty measures like confidence intervals or standard deviations for the performance metrics.

Item22.reason: The study does not assess the calibration of the final model.

Item23.reason: The study does not perform uni-parametric evaluations to justify the need for a multi-parametric approach.

Item24.reason: The study compares the performance of the PCMM-Net with clinico-radiological features assessed by radiologists, which represents a non-radiomic approach.

Item25.reason: The study does not compare the performance with a simple baseline reference model.

Item26.reason: The model is tested on an independent validation dataset sampled from the same source as the training data.

Item27.reason: The model is not tested with independent data from other institutions.

Item28.reason: The study does not mention sharing any imaging, segmentation, clinical, or radiomics analysis data with the public.

Item29.reason: The study does not mention sharing any scripts related to automatic segmentation and/or modeling with the public.

Item30.reason: The study does not mention sharing the final model in any form with the public.
